issue_title: Undocumented required default constructor for types with type casters
issue_body:
## Issue description

When defining a custom type caster as per http://pybind11.readthedocs.io/en/master/advanced/cast/custom.html it is required that the type you are casting to/from has a default constructor. Upon careful consideration of the sample code, it is not surprising that this is the case, but it is not documented, and it gives a rather opaque template expansion error:

```
call to implicitly-deleted default constructor of 'value_conv'
```

## Reproducible example code

Do the same example as in the docs, but delete the default constructor.

```
struct inty { long long_value; inty(long v) : long_value(v) {} };
```

issue_title: Pass-by-reference is not supported for Sparse types
issue_body:
As described in the [passy-by-reference doc](http://pybind11.readthedocs.io/en/master/advanced/cast/eigen.html#pass-by-reference), I wonder:
1) is there any particular reason not to support ref for sparse types?
2) since not supported, if I have sparse matrix constructed in python via `scipy.sparse.csr_matrix`, then have to pass it by value to c++, would this bring much overhead if I have to do a mini-batch processing on the data?

issue_title: Pyd module linking to the intel mkl
Fail to update multi-PK objects
issue_body:
If you have a model with multiple PKs (which is supported by ldapdb), updating one of those models (in a `save()` operation fails if there are two objects where *one* of the PKs match, because when retrieving the old object for comparison, only one of the PK fields is used.

It seems that the DN could be used instead, as the object still carries the old DN (and manual DN changes aren't supported anyway).

issue_title: Apply debian template to views
Update pinned cryptography to 2.3
issue_body:
The security issue fixed in this release doesn't affect us, but we should upgrade anyway. To do this, the cryptography hashes in `letsencrypt-auto-source/pieces/dependency-requirements.txt ` should be updated and [letsencrypt-auto rebuilt](https://certbot.eff.org/docs/contributing.html#building-letsencrypt-auto-source-letsencrypt-auto).

We will also need to silence the deprecation warnings they added about `hmac.compare_digest`. This should be done using the `warnings` module.

issue_title: Provide neither an e-mail or --register-unsafely flag
issue_body:
1. Run the client with the `certonly` or `run` subcommands and a fresh config.
2. When asked for an e-mail, provide one that passes the clients `safe_email` test but will fail when sent to boulder. I used `<keyboard mashing>@<keyboard mashing>.com`.
3. When asked for an e-mail again, cancel. The client will register without an e-mail address.

issue_title: 'Path used multiple times' when migrating to v2
issue_body:
(Also on [Stackoverflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/45480401/path-used-multiple-times-when-migrating-to-google-cloud-enpoints-v2))

Hello

I tried to follow [the steps to migrate to v2](https://cloud.google.com/endpoints/docs/frameworks/legacy/v1/python/migrating) (appengine app) and faced what seems to be a bug.

I have multiple APIs under the same AppEngine service (pretty much like [here](https://github.com/cloudendpoints/endpoints-python/issues/47)), and changed my `app.yaml`, downloaded the `cloud-endpoints` library, put it in the `/lib` folder...

Then I pushed the code to AppEngine, using ` gcloud app deploy` only (at this time I did not know about `gcloud service-management`, we were not using this before).

When trying to call one of my endpoints then, I got this error 'ApiConfigurationError: POST path "synchro" used multiple times, in classes SQL and Synchro'

That's true, but those two methods are under differents APIs so it should work (it does, in v1).

My code

```
@endpoints.api(name="sql", version='v1.0')
class SQL(remote.Service):
   @endpoints.method(
        CREATE_SYNCHRO,
        message_types.VoidMessage,
        path='synchro',
        http_method='POST',
        name='synchros.start'
    )

@endpoints.api(name='synchro', version='v1.0')
class Synchro(remote.Service):
    @endpoints.method(
        DayUserList,
        UserList,
        path='synchro',
        http_method='POST',
        name='synchronisation'
    )
```
(So migrating from v1 to v2 does not seem to be as simple as Google says... :) )

issue_title: Create a Database versioning scheme
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

Until the first mainnet release, each raiden version is allowed to break compatibility with previous DB versions and thus require removing the database.

Naturally when we are on the mainnet this is not going to be a viable choice anymore so we need to introduce a DB versioning scheme and an upgrade mechanism.

At startup the raiden client should check the current version of the DB and if it's not equal to the latest version, then apply sequential DB updates.

## Task

This issue is only for the introduction of a DB versioning. The DB upgrade mechanism will depend on the schema changes introduced on each new version.

issue_title: Propagate alarm clock task exceptions
issue_body:
All exceptions that happen inside alarm clock tasks are ignored and only their stack trace is printed. This can lead to bugs being missed and should be avoided.

Any exception inside the alarm clock tasks **is a bug**. So we should [propagate](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/master/raiden/tasks.py#L101) all exceptions from the alarm tasks to the main task and let the exception be raised and quit the program.

issue_title: Interface for sos remove interface.
issue_body:
#543 

### Remove files and signatures
1. By default remove all specified files or dirs and their signatures
    `sos remove FILE_OR_DIR`
2. Remove tracked files (`-t`, `--tracked`) and their signatures
    `sos remove  --tracked`
3. Remove untracked files (`-u`, `--untracked`) (no signature)
    `sos remove  --untracked`
4. Remove signatures of all specified files (default to `.`)
    `sos remove  -s`

5. [Already the case] Remove signatures of tracked files
6. [Not meaningful] Remove signatures of untracked files
7. [Not useful because it can be done outside of sos] Remove files but not signatures.

### Remove tasks
1. Remove all or specified tasks (`--task`, `-t` has been used)
    `sos remove --tasks 12a`
2. Limit to status (`--status completed`, `-s` has been used )
    `sos remove --tasks --status completed failed`
3. Limit to age (`--time`, `-t` has been used, or `--age`)
     `sos remove --tasks --time 2d`

Perhaps we need a separate command for `sos remove --tasks`, something like

```
sos tasks IDs --remove
```

with 
```
sos tasks IDs
```
is the existing
```
sos status IDs
```

Or we can do
```
sos remove-tasks IDs
```

issue_title: Variable input resolutions for ResNets
issue_body:
The current implementation of the ResNet models has a fixed [7x7 average pooling](https://github.com/pytorch/vision/blob/master/torchvision/models/resnet.py#L110) layer before the final FC  layer.

If this was changed to `nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d(1)` it would allow for variable sized input batches (e.g. like the 320x320 batches used at test time in the 'Identity Mappings in Deep Residual Network' paper)? Although I'm not sure if this will incur a performance hit?

issue_title: Remove or replace Redis client retry mechanism.
issue_body:
Right now, whenever we submit an asynchronous command to Redis (from a C++ client), we keep around enough information to resend the command. If we do not receive an acknowledgement from the Redis server within 10 seconds, we reissue the command. Then we wait another 10 seconds, and if we still haven't heard back, we reissue the command. And so on forever. A lot of the code for this is in `src/common/state/table.cc`. You can tell that this is happening because one of the log files will have messages like the following (see #561 for a workload that currently causes this problem)

```
[WARN] (/Users/rkn/Workspace/ray/src/common/state/table.cc:85) retrying operation task_table_test_and_update, retry_count = -1
```

Usually when this start happening, the system will not recover from it and will retry infinitely.

As far as I can tell, there are 0 situations where reissuing the Redis command is the right thing to do. Either the Redis client's connection to the Redis server is still alive, in which case the command will eventually go through just fine without any retrying, or the connection is been dropped in which case the connection needs to be reestablished, and retrying won't help with that (currently we don't have a way of reestablishing the connection).

For these reasons, I'd suggest removing the retry mechanism.

#!/usr/bin/env bash

issue_title: Wheels on pypi do not contain static version
issue_body:
### Directions

When importing the package I get an error. It looks like it is calling some git commands and trying to create a version. Looking into the code, I think it is supposed to do this only when it is built and replace _version.py with a static version getter.

The windows wheels seem to have the static file, but the linux ones do not (at least the python 2.7 ones I looked at).



### Bug reports

__Steps to reproduce:__

1. Install ssh2_python-0.6.0-cp27-cp27m-manylinux1_x86_64.whl (newer versions have same problem also)
2. look at <your path to site-packages>/ssh2/_version.py

>  from ssh2.session import Session
path-to-site-packages/ssh2/__init__.py:2: in <module>
     __version__ = get_versions()['version']
path-to-site-packages/ssh2/_version.py:507: in get_versions
    pieces = git_pieces_from_vcs(cfg.tag_prefix, root, verbose)
path-to-site-packages/ssh2/_version.py:298: in git_pieces_from_vcs
    pieces["distance"] = int(count_out)  # total number of commits
TypeError: int() argument must be a string or a number, not 'NoneType'

__Expected behaviour:__ 
_version.py contains static version, does not require git to be installed

__Actual behaviour:__
_version.py makes calls to git when ssh2 is imported

__Additional info:__
Version 0.6.0
Platform: Centos 6
Python version: 2.7.14

issue_title: Several installing issue(I have fixed)
issue_body:
Today, when i tried to use the goose package developed by grangier, finally i found it doesnt support python 3.x. So i found this package.
1. i have to say the man who rename the package and main directory to goose3 is careless.
At line 30 in file `setup.py`, 
`version = load_source("version", os.path.join("goose", "version.py"))` 
should be 
`version = load_source("version", os.path.join("goose3", "version.py"))`
2. The similar error happens in MANIFEST.in then the first 2 lines 
`recursive-include goose/resources/images *
recursive-include goose/resources/text *` 
should be 
`recursive-include goose3/resources/images *
recursive-include goose3/resources/text` *`
3. When i tried to import this package, the console turned out `no module named goose3.utils`. So i supposed to add
`recursive-include goose3/extractors *
recursive-include goose3/utils *`
into MANIFEST.in sequencely at the end

After i changed the code above, it works!

issue_title: HOW to fix assertionError:0, while running this code on single GPU (GeForce GTX 1080 Ti)
issue_body:
#!/usr/bin/env python
# -*- coding: UTF-8 -*-

import numpy as np
import tensorflow as tf
import argparse
import os
import cv2

os.environ["CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES"]="0"
from tensorpack import *
from tensorpack.tfutils.symbolic_functions import *
from tensorpack.tfutils.summary import *
from tensorpack.utils.gpu import get_nr_gpu

"""
CIFAR10 DenseNet example. See: http://arxiv.org/abs/1608.06993
Code is developed based on Yuxin Wu's ResNet implementation: https://github.com/ppwwyyxx/tensorpack/tree/master/examples/ResNet
Results using DenseNet (L=40, K=12) on Cifar10 with data augmentation: ~5.77% test error.
Running time:
On one TITAN X GPU (CUDA 7.5 and cudnn 5.1), the code should run ~5iters/s on a batch size 64.
"""

BATCH_SIZE = 64

class Model(ModelDesc):
    def __init__(self, depth):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        self.N = int((depth - 4)  / 3)
        self.growthRate =12

    def _get_inputs(self):
        return [InputDesc(tf.float32, [None, 32, 32, 3], 'input'),
                InputDesc(tf.int32, [None], 'label')
               ]

    def _build_graph(self, input_vars):
        image, label = input_vars
        image = image / 128.0 - 1

        def conv(name, l, channel, stride):
            return Conv2D(name, l, channel, 3, stride=stride,
                          nl=tf.identity, use_bias=False,
                          W_init=tf.random_normal_initializer(stddev=np.sqrt(2.0/9/channel)))
        def add_layer(name, l):
            shape = l.get_shape().as_list()
            in_channel = shape[3]
            with tf.variable_scope(name) as scope:
                c = BatchNorm('bn1', l)
                c = tf.nn.relu(c)
                c = conv('conv1', c, self.growthRate, 1)
                l = tf.concat([c, l], 3)
            return l

        def add_transition(name, l):
            shape = l.get_shape().as_list()
            in_channel = shape[3]
            with tf.variable_scope(name) as scope:
                l = BatchNorm('bn1', l)
                l = tf.nn.relu(l)
                l = Conv2D('conv1', l, in_channel, 1, stride=1, use_bias=False, nl=tf.nn.relu)
                l = AvgPooling('pool', l, 2)
            return l


        def dense_net(name):
            l = conv('conv0', image, 16, 1)
            with tf.variable_scope('block1') as scope:

                for i in range(self.N):
                    l = add_layer('dense_layer.{}'.format(i), l)
                l = add_transition('transition1', l)

            with tf.variable_scope('block2') as scope:

                for i in range(self.N):
                    l = add_layer('dense_layer.{}'.format(i), l)
                l = add_transition('transition2', l)

            with tf.variable_scope('block3') as scope:

                for i in range(self.N):
                    l = add_layer('dense_layer.{}'.format(i), l)
            l = BatchNorm('bnlast', l)
            l = tf.nn.relu(l)
            l = GlobalAvgPooling('gap', l)
            logits = FullyConnected('linear', l, out_dim=10, nl=tf.identity)

            return logits

        logits = dense_net("dense_net")

        prob = tf.nn.softmax(logits, name='output')

        cost = tf.nn.sparse_softmax_cross_entropy_with_logits(logits=logits, labels=label)
        cost = tf.reduce_mean(cost, name='cross_entropy_loss')

        wrong = prediction_incorrect(logits, label)
        # monitor training error
        add_moving_summary(tf.reduce_mean(wrong, name='train_error'))

        # weight decay on all W
        wd_cost = tf.multiply(1e-4, regularize_cost('.*/W', tf.nn.l2_loss), name='wd_cost')
        add_moving_summary(cost, wd_cost)

        add_param_summary(('.*/W', ['histogram']))   # monitor W
        self.cost = tf.add_n([cost, wd_cost], name='cost')

    def _get_optimizer(self):
        lr = tf.get_variable('learning_rate', initializer=0.1, trainable=False)
        tf.summary.scalar('learning_rate', lr)
        return tf.train.MomentumOptimizer(lr, 0.9, use_nesterov=True)

def get_batch_factor():
    nr_gpu = get_nr_gpu()
    assert nr_gpu in [0,1, 2, 4, 8], nr_gpu
    return 8 // nr_gpu

def get_data(train_or_test):
    isTrain = train_or_test == 'train'
    ds = dataset.Cifar10(train_or_test)
    pp_mean = ds.get_per_pixel_mean()
    if isTrain:
        augmentors = [
            imgaug.CenterPaste((40, 40)),
            imgaug.RandomCrop((32, 32)),
            imgaug.Flip(horiz=True),
            #imgaug.Brightness(20),
            #imgaug.Contrast((0.6,1.4)),
            imgaug.MapImage(lambda x: x - pp_mean),
        ]
    else:
        augmentors = [
            imgaug.MapImage(lambda x: x - pp_mean)
        ]
    ds = AugmentImageComponent(ds, augmentors)
    ds = BatchData(ds, BATCH_SIZE, remainder=not isTrain)
    if isTrain:
        ds = PrefetchData(ds, 3, 2)
    return ds

def get_config():
    log_dir = 'train_log/cifar10-single-fisrt%s-second%s-max%s' % (str(args.drop_1), str(args.drop_2), str(args.max_epoch))
    logger.set_logger_dir(log_dir, action='n')

    # prepare dataset
    dataset_train = get_data('train')
    steps_per_epoch = dataset_train.size()
    dataset_test = get_data('test')

    return TrainConfig(
        dataflow=dataset_train,
        callbacks=[
            ModelSaver(),
            InferenceRunner(dataset_test,
                [ScalarStats('cost'), ClassificationError()]),
            ScheduledHyperParamSetter('learning_rate',
                                      [(1, 0.1), (args.drop_1, 0.01), (args.drop_2, 0.001)])
        ],
        model=Model(depth=args.depth),
        steps_per_epoch=steps_per_epoch,
        max_epoch=args.max_epoch,
    )

if __name__ == '__main__':
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    parser.add_argument('--gpu', help='comma separated list of GPU(s) to use.') # nargs='*' in multi mode
    parser.add_argument('--load', help='load model')
    parser.add_argument('--drop_1',default=150, help='Epoch to drop learning rate to 0.01.') # nargs='*' in multi mode
    parser.add_argument('--drop_2',default=225,help='Epoch to drop learning rate to 0.001')
    parser.add_argument('--depth',default=40, help='The depth of densenet')
    parser.add_argument('--max_epoch',default=300,help='max epoch')
    args = parser.parse_args()

    if args.gpu:
        os.environ['CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES'] = args.gpu

    config = get_config()
    if args.load:
        config.session_init = SaverRestore(args.load)
    
    nr_tower = 0
    if args.gpu:
        nr_tower = max(get_nr_gpu(),1)
    
    # SyncMultiGPUTrainer(config).train()
    launch_train_with_config(config, SyncMultiGPUTrainerParameterServer(nr_tower))

issue_title: tcp stream receive_some() is blocking when received data is present
issue_body:
I'm sending short strings to an echo websocket server on plain text.  The received data appears to get stuck and receive_some() blocks.  Observed on OS X.

If I use SSL connection, or use receive_some(1), the problem goes away.

Repro follows.  Websocket handling code is extracted from asyncwebsockets.

```
import trio
import yarl
from wsproto import events
from wsproto.connection import WSConnection, ConnectionType
from wsproto.events import ConnectionEstablished

counter = 0

async def next_event(sock, connection):
    while True:
        data = await sock.receive_some(4096)
        connection.receive_bytes(data)
        for event in connection.events():
            if isinstance(event, events.DataReceived):
                assert event.message_finished
            return event

async def writer(sock, connection):
    global counter
    values = ('A', 'B', 'C')
    counter += len(values)
    for query in values:
        print('-->', query)
        connection.send_data(payload=query, final=True)
        data = connection.bytes_to_send()
        await sock.send_all(data)

async def reader(sock, connection):
    while True:
        ev = await next_event(sock, connection)
        print('<--', ev.data)
        global counter
        counter -= 1
        if counter == 0:
            break

async def test():
    url = 'ws://echo.websocket.org'
    url = yarl.URL(url)
    addr = (url.host, int(url.port))
    path = url.path
    sock = await trio.open_tcp_stream(*addr)
    connection = WSConnection(ConnectionType.CLIENT, host=addr[0], resource=path)

    # start the handshake
    data = connection.bytes_to_send()
    await sock.send_all(data)

    ev = await next_event(sock, connection)
    assert isinstance(ev, ConnectionEstablished)

    async with trio.open_nursery() as nursery:
        nursery.start_soon(writer, sock, connection)
        nursery.start_soon(reader, sock, connection)

trio.run(test)
```

Successful run will output as follows:
```
--> A
--> B
--> C
<-- A
<-- B
<-- C
```
Bad run will hang for a long time at e.g. `<-- B`, waiting for the final reply.

issue_title: A quick review
issue_body:
## String vs module

The library is currently identified using a string:
```python
library = current_async_library()
if library == "trio": ...
```
What about using the module itself?
```python
import trio
[...]
library = current_async_library()
if library is trio: ...
```

## Support for older curio/trio releases

Current  or older curio/trio versions won't be detected. 

Maybe have a fallback to detect them (e.g using `trio.hazmat.current_task()`)?

If not, it'd be good to list the compatible versions of the different libraries in the documentation.

## Set context var after sniffing when possible

If the current loop implements PEP 567, the context var can be set after sniffing to avoid performing the detection several times in the same task. 

PEP 567 support might be complicated to detect though.

issue_title: jaeger-client cannot be installed on older versions of setuptools
issue_body:
Essentially, this is the issue: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/48048745/setup-py-require-a-recent-version-of-setuptools-before-trying-to-install

We use a `python_version` specifier in the requirements here: https://github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger-client-python/blob/3.8.0/setup.py#L38, and only recent versions of `setuptools` support this. If you try to install and your `setuptools` is older, it will fail.

Not sure what the best solution is here

issue_title: S3: can't download files uploaded with gzip content-encoding
issue_body:
**Steps to reproduce:**
1. run `docker-compose up` with `docker-compose.yml` attached below.
2. Create a bucket called "test-bucket"
3. Run `test_localstack_s3.sh`, attached below.

**Excpected behavior:**
The file gets downloaded as `downloaded_bla.json.gz`.

**Actual behavior:**
AWS CLI freezes for some time and then produces `download failed: s3://test-bucket/bla.json.gz to ./downloaded_bla.json.gz Max Retries Exceeded`. No logs are produced by the `localstack` container during this event.

**docker-compose.yml:**
```
---
version: '2'
services:
  aws:
    image: localstack/localstack:0.8.6
    ports:
      - "4572:4572"
    environment:
      - SERVICES=s3
```

**test_localstack_s3.sh:**
```
PORT=4572
BUCKET=test-bucket
set -v

echo '{"a": 123, "b": "qwert"}' > bla.json
gzip bla.json
aws s3 --endpoint-url=http://localhost:${PORT} cp \
    --content-type application/json --content-encoding gzip \
    bla.json.gz s3://${BUCKET}/
aws s3 --endpoint-url=http://localhost:${PORT} \
    cp s3://${BUCKET}/bla.json.gz downloaded_bla.json.gz
```

**More context:**
If I just ommit the `--content-encoding gzip` then everything works properly.

I saw in one issue report, that localstack has a `--debug` option, so I wanted to run it with that. But I couldn't install it with pip due to errors.

I also tried rebuilding the image so that it would start with the debug option, which I guess can be done by modifying the parameters (adding `--debug`) in the "infra" target in the Makefile, but I got errors when trying to build the image...

issue_title: Localstack boto conflict with awscli
issue_body:
Localstack and awscli cannot co-exist on the same system due to a boto version conflict.

boto3 1.7.45 has requirement botocore<1.11.0,>=1.10.45, but you'll have botocore 1.10.30 which is incompatible.
awscli 1.15.45 has requirement botocore==1.10.45, but you'll have botocore 1.10.30 which is incompatible.

issue_title: Run a model in distributed mode
issue_body:
Tensorflow Estimators allow to run models in data-parallel async way without changing code at all.

The way to configure the run, is by providing environment variable with configuration:
```
export TF_CONFIG='{"cluster": {
   "ps": ["ps-01:2222", "ps-02:2222"], 
   "worker": ["worker-01:2222", "worker-02:2222", 
              "worker-03:2222", "worker-04:2222"]},
   "task": {"type": "worker", "index": 1}}'
```

`studio run` should allow to configure a remote distributed run on spot instances by mediating cluster configuration to the workers. E.g. `studio run --workers=4 --ps=2 --cloud=gcloud myfile.py`.

issue_title: Add a layer that supports lambda expressions for multiple layers
issue_body:
### New Issue Checklist

- [ ] I have read the [Contribution Guidelines](https://github.com/tensorlayer/tensorlayer/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md)
- [T] I searched for [existing GitHub issues](https://github.com/tensorlayer/tensorlayer/issues)

### Issue Description

Add a new layer. Enter multiple layers as arguments to function operations.
It looks like a combination of ElementwiseLayer and LambdaLayer.
I think it can simplify some operations.

### Reproducible Code

```python

import tensorflow as tf
import tensorlayer as tl

class ElementwiseLambdaLayer(tl.layers.Layer):
    def __init__(
            self,
            layers,
            lambda_fn,
            name='elementwiselambda_layer',
    ):
        super(ElementwiseLambdaLayer, self).__init__(prev_layer=layers, name=name)
        ts = [layer.outputs for layer in layers]
        self.outputs = lambda_fn(*ts)
        self.all_layers.append(self.outputs)

def get_encoder(x, is_train, reuse):
    noise_t = tf.random_normal(tf.stack([tf.shape(x)[0], n_hidden]))
    with tf.variable_scope('encoder', reuse=reuse):
        net = tl.layers.InputLayer(x)
        net = tl.layers.FlattenLayer(net)
        net = tl.layers.DenseLayer(net, 400, tf.nn.elu, name='dense1')
        mean = tl.layers.DenseLayer(net, n_hidden, name='mean')
        std = tl.layers.DenseLayer(net, n_hidden, name='std')
        noise = tl.layers.InputLayer(noise_t, name='noise')
        # mean_t = mean.outputs
        # std_t = std.outputs
        # z = mean_t + noise_t * tf.exp(std_t * 0.5)
        z = ElementwiseLambdaLayer([mean, std, noise], lambda mean, std, noise: mean + noise * tf.exp(std * 0.5))
        return z, mean, std

issue_title: change config name from app.name to image.name
Add WheelBuilder and unpack_file shims
Solid Registry and config-driven solid definition
issue_body:
Right now we can "inject" solids into a sub_pipelne using `PipelineDefinition.create_single_solid_pipeline` and `PipelineDefinition.create_sub_pipeline`.

However we want to be able to do this with via config. In order to do this we will need to be able construct solids with only a string as a key. This will necessitate the ability to register solids and look them up by string and parameterize them. You can think of these as "higher order solids" in a way. The libraries need to declare a way to create solids, and then config can be set up to invoke those higher order solids to produce solids. This sounds complicate but when seen in practice it will be quite intuitive.

We want to be able specify something like

```yaml

injected_solids:
    solid_name:
        input_name:
           solid: load_pandas_df_from_csv
           args:
              delimiter: "|"
``` 

This config would dynamically create a solid which is injected into the pipeline.

issue_title: ImportError: No module named term
issue_body:
kaska...@gmail.com, 2011-09-27T23:17:41.000Z 
What steps will reproduce the problem?
1. hg clone https://code.google.com/p/rdflib/ 
2. mv rdflib/rdflib /tmp/extras
3. touch /tmp/extras/__init__.py
4. execute python from /tmp directory and execute: from extras import rdflib

What is the expected output? 
no output, just rdflib loaded

What do you see instead?
Python 2.7.1+ (r271:86832, Apr 11 2011, 18:13:53) 
[GCC 4.5.2] on linux2
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> from extras import rdflib
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "", line 1, in 
  File "extras/rdflib/__init__.py", line 64, in 
    from rdflib.term import URIRef, BNode, Literal, Variable
ImportError: No module named term


What version of the product are you using? On what operating system?
Using last source from hg repository on ubuntu 11.04

Please provide any additional information below.
Pending to test in other environments. I use to follow the same steps with previous version of rdflib (2.4) with no problem at all.
Thanks in advance. I love rdflib.

Comment 1 by gjhigg...@gmail.com
If I understand it correctly, your approach relies on Python<=2.5 behaviour in which relative imports were the default. In Python>=2.6 that default has been changed in favour of absolute imports.

I don't know of any crufty, encapsulating meta-package trick that would allow you to continue your strategy uninterrupted. I was able to confirm that serial replacement of "import rdflib.X" with "import .X" does eventually result in a successful import of rdflib but I wasn't able to take it any further than that.

If there are no other options available to you then the only suggestion I can make is that you create a separate clone of rdflib, edited to perform relative imports as described above (assuming that it satisfactorily executes your tests) and to use that clone for your testing.

Sorry I can't be of more help.


Comment 2 by kaska...@gmail.com
Thanks a lot for pointing me in the right way. I hadn't noticed this change in Python. I've been reading PEP 328 (Absolute and Relative Imports) and it seems there is nothing to do. As you say Python 2.5 was the last version allowing relative imports. 

It's not a big problem for me to use absolute imports but I was happiest building my own bundles and test them in other distros or shared webservers. 

I'm not an python expert but I wish if instead:
from __future__ import absolute_import
I could use:
from __past__ import relative_import


Much appreciated.


Comment 3 by gromgull
not really a bug.
